---
title: "Reproducing the CV R² Study Results"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Reproducing the CV R² Study Results}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Introduction

This vignette provides the steps and code required to reproduce the simulation and empirical findings presented in the paper "Evaluating the Reliability of Cross-Validated R² Estimates in Random-Effects Meta-Regression".

# Setup

```r
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(metafor)
```

# Reproducing Empirical Results

The empirical results are stored in the package's `extdata` directory. To regenerate the empirical summary table:

```r
data_path <- system.file("extdata", "paper4_empirical_FINAL.csv", package = "claude2")
empirical <- read.csv(data_path)
empirical_summary <- empirical %>%
  select(Dataset, k, Apparent_R2, CV_weighted, CV_unweighted) %>%
  mutate(Optimism = Apparent_R2 - CV_weighted)
print(empirical_summary)
```

# Reproducing Simulation Summary

To observe the effect of $k$ on optimism:

```r
sim_data_path <- system.file("extdata", "paper4_simulation_summary.csv", package = "claude2")
sim_summary <- read.csv(sim_data_path)
optimism_by_k <- sim_summary %>%
  group_by(k) %>%
  summarize(mean_optimism = mean(optimism_w_mean, na.rm = TRUE))
print(optimism_by_k)
```

# Visualization

The figures in the paper can be inspected in the `figures/` directory. For example, `figures/paper4_fig1_cv_divergence_FINAL.png` illustrates the primary findings regarding the gap between apparent and CV estimates.
